description:
You are a mobile build-pipeline engineer.  
For a quick smoke-check you need to keep a tiny, local SQLite registry of builds and then generate a one-line text report from it.

Required end state of the workstation:

1. A brand-new SQLite database named
   /home/user/build_stats.db

2. Inside that database a single table named `builds`
   with **exactly** the following schema (column order matters):
        id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT  
        branch    TEXT  
        status    TEXT  
        duration  INTEGER

3. One and only one record must be present in the table, with these exact values:  
        id = 1  
        branch = "develop"  
        status = "success"  
        duration = 12

4. A plain-text report file located at  
   /home/user/build_report.txt  
   whose entire contents is precisely (including the trailing newline):
        Total successful builds: 1⏎        ← note the single newline at the end

Additional requirements and hints:

• Use only the `sqlite3` command-line client that is pre-installed in the container; Python or other wrappers are not allowed.  
• The report file must be produced by executing an SQL query against the database, **not** by hard-coding the text.  
• Do all work as the normal user in /home/user; no sudo actions are necessary.  
• When you are finished, running `cat /home/user/build_report.txt` must show exactly the one line shown above, and querying the database with  
        sqlite3 /home/user/build_stats.db "SELECT * FROM builds;"  
  must return:  
        1|develop|success|12

These two checks are what the automated grader will run, so make sure the file paths, table name, column order, and data values match exactly.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. File: /home/user/build_stats.db  
   Verification steps:  
   • Contains one table `builds`.  
   • `PRAGMA table_info(builds);` returns (in order):  
        0|id|INTEGER|0||1  
        1|branch|TEXT|0||0  
        2|status|TEXT|0||0  
        3|duration|INTEGER|0||0  
   • `SELECT * FROM builds;` returns exactly one row:  
        1|develop|success|12

2. File: /home/user/build_report.txt  
   Exact byte contents (including final newline, shown here as \n):  
        "Total successful builds: 1\n"
name: task_000000_de71fbca
